sourceMovie.title: Mirrors
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mirrors works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Scream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mirrors clicked for you, Scream (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mirrors?
faq.answer: Try Scream, Scream 2, Smile—each shares DNA with Mirrors while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mirrors?
faq.answer: Mirrors (2008) is best described as Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mirrors part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Mirrors Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
